frameworks.name: multi-factor account of visual illusions
frameworks.one_line: each illusion constitutes its own factor rather than sharing a common cause

frameworks.name: common-factor account of visual illusions
frameworks.one_line: single latent factor purported to underlie all visual illusions

frameworks.name: subclass account of visual illusions
frameworks.one_line: illusions group into a few classes such as linear-extent or distortion

frameworks.name: psychophysics of individual differences
frameworks.one_line: stable per-observer perceptual traits assessed via test-retest reliability

core_question: Are individual differences in visual illusion magnitudes stable across eyes, days, and psychophysical methods?
key_assumptions: illusion magnitude is a measurable per-individual trait
staircase procedures yield comparable magnitude estimates
within- vs between-illusion variance partitioning indexes shared structure
key_findings: each illusion's magnitude is highly stable per individual across eye, time, and method
interpretations: results support a high-dimensional multifactorial space where each illusion is largely its own factor
evidence argues against a single common factor or coarse subclass account
